stimulus: Close analysis of the painting Mountain Lake at Dusk, long attributed to the nineteenth-century landscape painter Caroline Whitford, has revealed subtle departures from the artist's well-documented brushwork. These differences suggest that the work may be that of an apprentice working under Whitford's supervision—potentially ______blank our understanding of Whitford as a wholly self-sufficient painter.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "Undermining" most logically completes the text. If a painting attributed to Whitford was actually made by an apprentice, the view that she worked entirely alone would be weakened—undermined—by this evidence.

Choice A is incorrect. "Entrenching" means firmly establishing; an apprentice's contribution would weaken, not strengthen, the view of Whitford as wholly self-sufficient. Choice B is incorrect. "Substantiating" means confirming, again contradicting the implications of the apprentice hypothesis. Choice C is incorrect. "Prefiguring" means foreshadowing, which doesn't match the relationship of new evidence to an existing view.
